meaning: adj.有资格领退休金的,可领退休金的
examples: Entitlements can be cut back, most obviously by raising pensionable ages.
Delaying their retirement by raising the pensionable age might make sense.
So far only Denmark has taken the radical step of indexing the pensionable age to life expectancy.
More recently, the number of people of pensionable age who are at work has also risen.
In America, for example, the normal pensionable age, fixed at 65 in 1935, is due to rise to 67.
